content: Remote OK Senior Manager Government & Industry Affairs Operations VGW Senior Manager, Government & Industry Affairs OperationsVGW is an interactive entertainment company, harnessing technology and creativity to deliver world-class, free-to-play online social games.We have an exciting opportunity to join our Government & Industry Affairs team in the United States and are currently looking for a Senior Manager, Government & Industry Affairs Operations to join the team. This is a newly created role that will play an important part in helping VGW coordinate and execute its government affairs activity across the US. You will be responsible for bringing structure, discipline, and clear operating rhythm to a complex, multi state lobbying network, ensuring our external partners are well coordinated, accountable, and aligned to business priorities.You will work closely with Government Affairs, Legal, Marketing, and external lobbying partners to ensure activity is well planned, compliant, and clearly tracked.Weâre open to candidates based anywhere in the US, and this role can be performed remotely. We do not currently have a US office, but occasional in person office attendance may be required if that changes in future.The salary range for this role is USD $200,000 - $230,000 per year.Key responsibilities will include:Managing the day to day operations of VGWâs external lobbying contractors, ensuring activity is clearly understood, tracked, and delivered.Supporting compliance across VGWâs lobbying activity, including oversight of relevant registrations, disclosures, and state and federal requirements.Establishing a single source of truth for legislative tracking, reporting, and key activity across the lobbying network.Building dashboards, reporting frameworks, and quarterly operating reviews tied to clear KPIs for lobbying firms.Developing manager, senior, operations Maryland 2026-05-08T14:00:48+00:00 1131518 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-senior-manager-government-industry-affairs-operations-vgw-1131518 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png People Operations Lead Defense Unicorns Role Description: As a People Operations Lead, you will serve as the operational backbone and strategic right hand of the Chief People Officer, owning the infrastructure that makes our people programs run while serving as a trusted partner to leaders and employees across every department.This is a company-wide role that lives at the intersection of HR operations, people strategy, and organizational partnership. You will build and improve the systems, processes, and programs that shape the Defense Unicorns employee experience from day one through every milestone that follows. This is not a support role â it is a high-trust, high-visibility position for someone who moves fast, thinks critically, and takes action: identifies gaps, takes ownership, and drives solutions forward without waiting to be directed.At Defense Unicorns, the order of priority is: 1) People 2) Culture 3) Process 4) Technology.We champion our values, are open to experimentation, take ownership, and have fun doing it. As a fast-growing company, policies and processes are continuously being defined and refined and this person will be a key architect of that work, not just a follower of it.Responsibilities: Company-Wide People LeadershipNavigate complex employee situations with sound judgment, ensuring outcomes are fair, timely, and compliant with applicable laws and regulations.Support managers in building high-perfo hr, architect, support United States 2026-05-08T14:00:34+00:00 1131517 https://remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/remote-people-operations-lead-defense-unicorns-1131517 https://remoteOK.com/assets/logo-square.png Web Development Specialist Marketers in Demand <h1>Overview<br /></h1><p>We're looking for a Web Development Specialist to build and maintain marketing websites and landing pages for our agency's B2B technology clients. This is a hands-on production role across the three platforms our clients live on most: WordPress, HubSpot CMS (COS), and Webflow.<br />You should be equally comfortable building a custom WordPress theme, standing up a HubSpot CMS site with HubL templates and modules, and shipping a Webflow build with a clean CMS structure. On top of that, the modern web is increasingly about wiring things togetherâso we want someone who's strong with APIs and can move fast using the latest AI-assisted build tools (Lovable, Replit, v0, Cursor, Bolt) when the situation calls for it.<br />The work spans new site builds, ongoing iteration on existing client sites, landing pages for paid campaigns, and the integrations that connect those pages to the rest of the marketing stack (CRM, analytics, automations).</p><h1>Core Responsibilities</h1><ul><li>Build and maintain client sites across WordPress, HubSpot CMS (COS), and Webflow</li><li>Develop custom WordPress themes, blocks, and ACF-powered layouts</li><li>Build HubSpot CMS templates, modules, and themes using HubL</li><li>Build Webflow sites with well-structured CMS collections and reusable components</li><li>Translate Figma designs into pixel-accurate, responsive, performant pages</li><li>Integrate with third-party APIsâHubSpot, Salesforce, Stripe, Airtable, custom webhooks</li><li>Build and connect forms, lead routing, and automation flows across CRM and marketing tools</li><li>Use AI-assisted build platforms (Lovable, Replit, v0, Cursor, Bolt) to prototype and ship faster</li><li>Implement analytics, conversion tracking, and tag management (GA4, GTM, HubSpot)</li><li>Optimize for Core Web Vitals, accessibility, and SEO fundamentals</li><li>Collaborate with designers, strategists, and paid media specialists to ship fast</li></ul><h1>Requirements</h1><ul><li>Strong hands-on experience with WordPress (custom themes, ACF, common page builders)</li><li>Strong hands-on experience with HubSpot CMS / COS (HubL templates, modules, themes)</li><li>Strong hands-on experience with Webflow (CMS, interactions, structured builds)</li><li>Solid working knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and PHP</li><li>Comfortable integrating with REST/GraphQL APIsâreading docs, handling auth, debugging payloads</li><li>Experience using modern AI-assisted build platforms like Lovable, Replit, v0, Cursor, or Bolt</li><li>Familiarity with GA4, Google Tag Manager, and conversion tracking setup</li><li>Understanding of responsive design, performance, and accessibility basics</li><li>Self-managed: can run multiple projects in parallel and hit deadlines</li></ul><h1>Nice to Have</h1><ul><li>Experience with B2B or technology brands specifically</li><li>Familiarity with Next.js / React for occasional custom builds outside the three core platforms</li><li>Experience with Zapier, Make, n8n, or similar low-code automation tools</li><li>Light backend or serverless experience (API routes, edge functions, custom endpoints)</li><li>Familiarity with Sanity, Contentful, or other headless CMS platforms</li><li>Experience building reusable component libraries or template systems</li></ul><h1>What We're Looking For<br /></h1><p>A fast, pragmatic builder who's fluent across WordPress, HubSpot, and Webflow and treats APIs as a first-class tool, not a last resort. The right person knows when to reach for a CMS, when to build a custom integration, and when to use a vibe-coding platform like Lovable or Replit to prototype something in an afternoon.
